sections.heading: Lead
sections.content:
Xiahou Yuan (onyomi: Kakō En) is one of Cao Cao's cousins from his father's side of the family. Serving Cao Cao for his entire military career, his lord favored Xiahou Yuan for his humble behavior and his sheer guile in war. He served in many of his lord's campaigns until he abruptly perished to the rear attack led by Huang Zhong's army. Historically, the general was praised for his remarkable speed in war and for his expertise for ambushes. Romance of the Three Kingdoms is famous for portraying him as a masterful archer and stressed that Xiahou Dun treated him as though he were his younger brother. Xiahou Ba is one of his sons and Xiahou Ji is his niece.

Dynasty Warriors Xiahou Yuan is twenty-sixth place with fans in Gamecity's Dynasty Warriors 7 character popularity poll and thirty-eighth in the Dynasty Warriors 8: Xtreme Legends poll. In Famitsu's character survey, he placed in two categories: eighth place for friend and tied with second place for most desired boss. The latest poll for the eighth installment puts him in twenty-first. The character poll for overseas fans puts him in nineteenth place for the Wei division.

In Kessen II, his character's height is 180 cm (5'11").

In 211, Xiahou Yuan joined Cao Cao in suppressing Ma Chao's revolt at Tong Gate and defeated him. Afterwards, he and Zhu Ling led their forces to pacify the Di tribes in Liang Province and joined Cao Cao at Anding, where they forced Yang Qiu to surrender. He was appointed "General who Protects the Army" and was put in charge of defending Chang'an. He defeated the bandit leader Liu Xiong and later besieged and killed Liang Xing at Hu.

sections.heading: Role in Games
sections.content:
"Hey, big guy, you're the true champion around here!"
~~Magoichi; Warriors Orochi 2

~~Magoichi; Warriors Orochi 2

sections.heading: Dynasty Warriors
sections.content:
Xiahou Yuan is one of Cao Cao's prized generals who appears in many of his cousin's early campaigns. During the Battle of Guan Du, he sometimes scouts Zhang He's talents as an asset for Cao Cao. Unless it is his own story, he often dies against his rival at Mt. Ding Jun if left unattended.

His Legend Mode in Dynasty Warriors 4: Xtreme Legends focus on an incident during the Battle of Mt. Ding Jun. Zhang He lost foothold of his position and fled back to Xiahou Yuan wounded. Forgiving his comrade's fault, he personally rides into battle to defend the main camp from Huang Zhong's soldiers. They fight in a narrow pass while Zhang He recovers. As time progresses, Xiahou Dun reinforces him and they endure the Shu general's assault until Cao Cao arrives.

Dynasty Warriors 5 describes him as a masterful general who specializes in archery and surprise attacks. He aids in ending his lord's rivals at Xia Pi and Guan Du. Following the latter battle, Xiahou Yuan helps his lord prevail at Chi Bi, even though the Allied forces survive the conflict. After their victory at Tong Gate, he is given the task of dealing with Liu Bei's advance at Mt. Ding Jun. He continues to act as the main commander in Cao Cao's absence and they are able to claim victory with their lord's reinforcements. Xiahou Yuan's bravery during the conflict weakens the resolve of Wei's rivals and the land is soon united under Cao Cao. Though living in a time of peace, Xiahou Yuan swears to never abandon his bow to honor those who were slain by it.

He shares his Legend Mode in the Xtreme Legends expansion with Zhang He, Cao Ren, and Cao Cao at the Battle of Yang Ping Gate. They face the supernatural Five Bushel sect led by Zhang Lu. Needing the area to open a path to Shu, Xiahou Yuan heads up the eastern mountain path to surprise Zhang Lu. If his ambush succeeds, the sorcerer will be flustered and cowers within his main camp.

Xiahou Yuan's ending scene in Dynasty Warriors: Online has him sit down talking to the player like an old friend. Concerned with the wounds and emotional trauma his companion sustained during the chaos, he assures them that their scars will someday be badges of honor and is glad to be their partner.

In Dynasty Warriors 6, Xiahou Yuan appears as a Free Mode only character. He appears to reinforce Xu Chang's defenses against Sun Ce and charges to reclaim one of their forts from Huang Zhong at Mt. Ding Jun. Based on his repeated appearances in the characters' ending scenarios, he survives the battle during Wei's story and sees the new land their lord envisioned. In Zhang He's story in Special, they work together to defend the mountain.

During Wei's Story Mode in Dynasty Warriors 7, Xiahou Yuan is one of the first generals who serve Cao Cao and appears in the story since the Yellow Turban Rebellion. He works together with Xiahou Dun during their start, seen saving Cao Cao from Dong Zhuo in the capital. The player controls him for the faction's participation at Sishui Gate and Hulao Gate. He personally reports to Cao Cao when they lose their footing in Yan Province to Lu Bu. To counter, Cao Cao orders him to march out and support Xiahou Dun. Xiahou Yuan is one of the generals who assist Cao Cao's escape from Chibi.

After recovering his forces from Chibi, Cao Cao orders Xiahou Yuan to become the main commander for the conquest of Hanzhong. Together with Zhang He, they begin their journey south by first attacking Yangping Gate. Over time, they face Liu Bei's forces at Mt. Dingjun. Xiahou Yuan feels something off about the battle this time around, but he decides to personally lead the men to take Huang Zhong's position. During the battle, he offers support and advice for Guo Huai. He prevails against his archer rival and orders his nearby guards to take him prisoner. Directing his attention to the messenger reporting Cao Cao's reinforcements, Xiahou Yuan is surprised when Huang Zhong kills his men and yells for another showdown. When each archer draws their arrow, Xiahou Yuan's bow breaks apart from its strain. As Xiahou Yuan looks on in disbelief, Huang Zhong shoots his arrow and hits his target. Falling from the blow, the Wei general spends his last moments uttering on how unlucky he is and dies. His death is reported to Cao Cao and greatly moves the entire Wei army, making it a painful withdraw from their mountain fort. On the other hand, during Shu's Story Mode, Xiahou Yuan unceremoniously dies in battle after losing twice to Huang Zhong.

His first Legendary Battle takes place after Tong Gate. Though defeated, Ma Chao urges his men to lead another charge against Cao Cao's army in Xiliang. Since they are guarding the area, Xiahou Yuan and Zhang He work together to suppress him. Players fight at Mt. Dingjun again for his second Legendary Battle, this time letting Xiahou Yuan have the chance to survive and claim victory over Huang Zhong.

In Dynasty Warriors Next, Xiahou Yuan arrives with Cao Cao as reinforcements for the imperial forces at Jinyang. During the Battle of Sishui Gate, he advises his lord to capture the enemy's armory and use their own ballistae against them. The fourth chapter has him be the player's avatar in Xiapi where he personally subdues Lu Bu while leading the charge against the latter's main camp. When Wu initiates their fire attack in Chibi, Xiahou Yuan stalls them long enough for Cao Cao to escape the burning wreckage. His rivalry with Huang Zhong occurs in the middle of Wei and Shu's last scenarios. Regardless of whether Xiahou Yuan or his opponent take Mt. Dingjun for their respective factions, both versions have the former survive the encounter. In Wei's ending, he and Xu Zhu invite their lord to see the new rice fields they planted together.

His role from the seventh title is retained in Dynasty Warriors 8. He starts early in the story supporting Cao Cao since the assassination attempt against Dong Zhuo. He also participates in many campaigns against the likes of Lu Bu, Zhang Xiu and other warlords. During the campaign against Lu Bu, he and Yue Jin kill Cao Xing who shot Xiahou Dun's eye with an arrow. He later participates in the defeat at Chibi along with the fight against Ma Chao before guarding Hanzhong. Despite defeating Zhuge Liang alongside many of his allies, he is caught off-guard by Huang Zhong and is fatally shot. Should the player avert his death in the historic route, he will join the showdown against Guan Yu at Fan Castle.

In the hypothetical route, Xiahou Yuan helps defend Xuchang from Zuo Ci's assault, and joins Cao Cao as he defeats Liu Bei at Baidi Castle.

Dynasty Warriors 8: Xtreme Legends has him try to shoot Liu Bei while near death at Shu's new version of Mt. Dingjun. The shot is taken by Fa Zheng who either dies or survives depending on whether or not the hypothetical conditions have been met.

In Wei's Xtreme Legend stages, his army struggles against the Shu forces only to be rescued by their lord together with Dian Wei and Guo Jia in "Mt. Dingjun Rescue Mission". Xiahou Yuan leads his men from Hanzhong to counterattack Shu in "Battle of Zitong" with Zhang He and Wang Yi to open a path to Chengdu. In "Find the Beauties" He gathers a search party composing of Yue Jin, Li Dian, and Yu Jin to find Cai Wenji on the frontiers while humorously running into every other female as kidnappers by mistake.

In Dynasty Warriors: Unleashed, Xiahou Yuan is the final boss of the Mt. Dingjun campaign. He will attempt to stack him himself with buffs provided by two special units: defense bonuses from imperial officers and healing from chief medics. Defeating these troops is essential to rendering him vulnerable to damage.

Xiahou Yuan's bond story recounts his various exploits as one of Cao Cao's chief retainers. When Yu Jin broods over his failure to defeat Chang Xi, Xiahou Yuan tells the strict general to lighten up and even helps him subjugate his target, earning themselves promotions upon their return. Despite receiving praise from Zhang He for being proficient in ambushes, he remains modest about his own abilities, thinking that most tacticians would find his methods too unorthodox for their liking.

Fondly remembering the time Cao Cao saved him and Xiahou Dun in the past, he is more than happy to repay that kindness through valor. While Xiahou Dun is concerned that acting on impulse will lead him to an early grave, it fails to dissuade Xiahou Yuan from being true to himself.

In Dynasty Warriors 9, Xiahou Yuan continues to reprise his previous roles. Just before the battle over Hanzhong, Xiahou Yuan is surprisingly greeted by his niece, who at that point, had married Zhang Fei. Though aware of her true loyalties, the two nonetheless enjoy their reunion, but Xiahou Yuan promises at the end to protect Hanzhong for his lord. Though Liu Bei is pushed back, Xiahou Yuan is hit by Huang Zhong's ambush at the main camp. Heavily surrounded and overrun by the enemy cavalry, Xiahou Yuan accepts his death at the hands of the elder general.

He appears prominently as an enemy in Xiahou Ji's personal DLC scenario, where he opposes her decision to aid Zhang Fei at Changban and Chibi. When he is killed at the Hanzhong campaign, the maiden and her husband secretly have the body given a proper burial. Despite being on opposite sides, Zhang Fei remembers the general fondly, noting that he will miss having to meet each other in the battlefield.

sections.heading: Warriors Orochi
sections.content:
In Orochi's story mode, Xiahou Yuan is one of the main generals who participated in his kingdom's resistance against the serpent king at Yi Ling. When the army is faced with cannons, however, he and the other generals flee to regain their composure. Eventually, he is defeated and he escapes capture.

Filled with pride for his kingdom, he joins an anti-Orochi resistance with Huang Gai and Xu Zhu in Warriors Orochi. When he sees Cao Pi in the enemy ranks, he chastises the heir for ruining Wei. He defiantly joins forces with his brother in an effort to defeat their lord's son. As they fight, both brothers realize Cao Pi's true intentions to rebel and finally join his ranks.

In Warriors Orochi 2, Xiahou Yuan leads the fire attack against Lu Bu's army at Si Shui Gate. At Ji Castle, he rescues Magoichi and Toshiie from being defeated by Sima Yi's offensive forces. He holds the fort with reserve forces while Magoichi rushes to gather his renegades. Though allied with her in this title, he has a hard time believing in Kunoichi's strength. In his Dream Mode stage, he teams up with Huang Zhong and Ina to show that archers can still surpass advanced military firearms at Honnōji while defending Nobunaga.

During the original timeline in Warriors Orochi 3, Xiahou Yuan once led a battalion of men and defended Nanjun. After an encounter with the serpent army, however, his whereabouts became unknown. To rescue the general from his fate, future Xiahou Ba and company return to the past to save him. Although their story about time travel baffles him, a perplexed Xiahou Yuan then fights for the coalition.

During Ultimate, he returns back to serving Wei. Due to the chaos created by Kyūbi's tails, his unit is attacked by Musashi's forces at Yan Provice after the swordsman is deceived by a fake Zhang Jiao. In the Mirror Realm version of the stage, he can approach and expose Zhang Jiao, causing Musashi to desert the fake and aid the Wei forces.

Xiahou Yuan was with Cao Cao when their party was sent to the new world in Warriors Orochi 4. Caught within a battle between Ares and Lu Bu at Nagashino, they are rescued by Liu Bei and Guan Yu's timely reinforcements and successfully repulse both armies. After being briefed at the events of the world, Xiahou Yuan joins his lord in becoming a part of the Coalition.

When Zeus is killed for Ares and Odin to set their sights on the Coalition, Xiahou Yuan assists in the escape of his son and Cao Pi. He also thanks Lady Hayakawa for taking in his son during his absence during the battle.

sections.heading: Kessen
sections.content: Xiahou Yuan is a powerful yet short-lived general in Kessen II. His War stat is high and his officer skills can easily rout any of Shu's generals. He is one of Cao Cao's greatest generals who provides strategies for battle. While they were riding to do battle with Zhuge Liang, both he and Cao Cao are caught in an ambush. Xiahou Yuan volunteers to take his lord's place alone on the field. Though his cousin protested, he retreats back to their base for a war council. Wei would later receive news of his death at Zhang Fei's hands.

sections.heading: Romance of the Three Kingdoms
sections.content:
Within the Romance of the Three Kingdoms series, Xiahou Yuan is depicted as a superior officer in military affairs with a War stat consistently in the low 90's while possessing a Charisma stat in the high 70's with his Intelligence and Politics stat being in the 50's to low 60's ranges. Notably, recent games in the series after the eleventh instalment see his Leadership stat rise from the high 80's to the low 90's placing him among the best in that category. An active member of Cao Cao's forces from the moment he raises an army in opposition of Dong Zhuo, Xiahou Yuan can be found as a free officer around Xuchang in scenarios predating the establishment of Cao Cao's faction.

Romance of the Three Kingdoms XIV places particular emphasis on Xiahou Yuan's qualities as a swift and valorous, but reckless general excelling in offense. His character traits increase the troop count damage his unit inflicts on cities and gates (Longbow), increase the mobility of his unit (Dash) and prevents his unit from being inflicted with the restrained ailment (Dauntless). Unfortunately, his remaining two traits prevent his unit from not engaging enemy units upon contact (Reckless) and increase the damage his unit receives from traps (Straightforward). The range of formations Xiahou Yuan has access to include the swift and aggressive Arrow formation, the ranged Goose formation, the terrain-adept Snake formation and the very fast Awl formation. This range of formations complements Xiahou Yuan's emphasis on swift, uninhibited offense within all terrains while also allowing him to support other units from range when required. His administrative policy bolsters the strength of the Goose formation significantly (Strengthen Goose Formation Lv 05). His unique battle skill, Flawless Arrow inflicts damage on enemy units while also reducing their manoeuvrability.

100man-nin no Sangokushi has a historical episode event featuring his reinforcements for Ji Castle, and his side of the story continues from Wang Yi's perspective. Speeding as fast as his men can from Hanzhong, Xiahou Yuan single-handedly defeats any enemy officer standing in their path. He swiftly defeats Ma Dai, the overseer for his cousin's authority over Cao Cao's loyalists, to free the castle. After a quick debriefing from Wang Yi, the general decides it is prudent to pursue Ma Dai in order to locate Ma Chao. Pang De duels him to bide time for Ma Chao's escape, but Xiahou Yuan stays true to his mission and routs Ma Chao. He and his forces struggle with the veteran Han Sui, yet they prevail and subjugate the rebels in Liangzhou.

sections.heading: Character Information
sections.content: Xiahou Yuan's Shin Sangoku Musou 15th Anniversary artwork.

sections.heading: Personality
sections.content:
Not one for eloquence, Xiahou Yuan is a gruff yet practical man of war. Though dedicated to his family's vision, he is more social and loose than his older cousins, willing to crack affable jokes during battle. Always on the go, Xiahou Yuan thinks of every battle as a step in Cao Cao's conquests and wants them to be done and over with as soon as possible. He has little tolerance for interruptions and is known to have a short fuse when the enemy gains the advantage. When off duty, he is actually a kind soul who doesn't stress too much over his losses. He is the one who offers an optimistic and friendly view to his brooding comrades within Wei while making light of rough situations at times. Occasionally, his softer side surfaces on the battlefield, as he remarks on his reluctance to fight against women, children, or men with good character (traits that all surface much more during and after Dynasty Warriors 4).

In the overseas script, Xiahou Yuan talks with a quick, lively rhythm and often says lines that are slightly more compassionate than his English counterpart, using such tics as the ending tic "da ze" and the more pompous "oresama" (俺様) pronoun to refer to himself; both of these traits make him stand out as not truly fitting in with the usual self-conceited archetype of person who uses such speech patterns, often with a bright smile on his face.

He admires Xiahou Dun and fondly calls him "Brother Dun" (惇兄). He shares his cousin's sense of loyalty and rudeness, as they can both be equally cold to their foes. Later installments simply has him refer to his relative as "cousin" in the English script. His relationship with his son, while rather playful, can occasionally turn awkward as the more outspoken father often makes remarks to the more cynical son, and their relationship appears more like one between brothers than between a father and son. He is also respected by Cao Cao, as his lord addresses Xiahou Yuan by his style name. Xiahou Yuan is amused by Zhang He's eccentric traits and is the only officer in Wei to openly criticize his "weirdness", though at the same time he respects Zhang He's abilities and knows that he is still a valuable ally as the two seem to be close as friends. This trait continues in the Warriors Orochi series when he meets some of the off-beat characters from the Samurai Warriors cast, such as Kunoichi and Yoshimoto. He is nicer to Ina, Nuwa, and Keiji in the same series.

If an original character decides to stay close to him, Xiahou Yuan's only request is for them to never die before him. Should he fall in battle, he asks that his comrade not follow him. Although he initially thinks it's a joke that a woman would want to be with him, Xiahou Yuan treasures his wife in Dynasty Warriors 6: Empires and frets over her well-being.

Highly confident in his own abilities, he is also known to tease Huang Zhong's age and capabilities when they clash at Mt. Dingjun. The two men have a rivalry throughout the series as masters of the bow, yet ironically they are shown to gradually get along in the Warriors Orochi series, with his "old man" nicknaming being more affectionate as time goes on. Additionally a cynic within the aforementioned crossover series, he expresses his doubts towards Himiko and Mitsunari's strengths.

In the second and third games, he is given a small rivalry with Magoichi, who ironically calls the general as an old man, and the two compete in marksmanship despite their difference in weapons. The fourth installment has him offer special praise to Muneshige's skills.

sections.heading: Character Symbolism
sections.content:
In the Japanese version of Dynasty Warriors 4: Empires, Xiahou Yuan is given the nickname of "The Swift Vanguard" while the English version changes it to "The Lightning Strike on the Battlefield". As a dominant ruler in Dynasty Warriors 5: Empires, he calls his five greatest warriors the "Five Arrows of Xiahou".

His third, fourth and fifth weapons in the third installment are named after a dog's fang; within Chinese mythology, dogs are often tied to being loyal partners of heroes, as well as having ties to agriculture. However, accounts have changed throughout the years during the Han period. The fourth weapon in particular is named after a dog of divinity, most likely referencing various divine dogs in Chinese mythology such as Panhu (lit. Platter Gourd), the iconic tiangou/tengu (lit. heaven dog), and perhaps even Erlang Shen's iconic dog, Xiaotianquan (lit. Howling Heaven Hound); Xiahou Yuan being associated with a dog may also represent his loyalties and ties with Cao Cao, along with his fierceness.

As of the fourth installment however, the names for his third and fourth weapons as well as his weapons in Dynasty Warriors 6 take their namesakes from the Nine Heavens. In modern times, this term is used to describe the astronomical objects rotating in our part of the galaxy: the sun, Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. During Ancient China, the concept refers to the heavens divided into the cardinal directions and their intermediate points.

Each heaven is protected by various deities within Daoism and continue to influence other aspects of Chinese mythology, including the Four Symbols; the said heaven motif also seems to tie to the motif towards his cousin, Cao Cao. As a foil to Huang Zhong's new weapon set in the said installments, Xiahou Yuan's club severs apart the opposition instead of slashing them.

His original fifth weapon references the term Fengshen, while his mystic weapon in Warriors Orochi 3 Ultimate references the Zhuque/Suzaku.

- Center - Juntian (鈞天, lit. Focal Heaven)

- East - Cangtian (蒼天, lit. Pale Heaven)

- West - Haotian (昊天, lit. Vast Heaven)

- South - Yantian (炎天, lit. Flame Heaven)

- North - Xuantian (玄天, lit. Occult Heaven)

- Northeast - Biantian (変天, lit. Changing Heaven)

- Northwest - Youtian (幽天, lit. Faint Heaven)

- Southwest - Zhutian (朱天, lit. Vermilion Heaven)

- Southeast - Yangtian (陽天, lit. Yang Heaven)

sections.heading: Quotes
sections.content:
- "I'm not gonna ask for help, but someone better offer soon!"

- "Come on... it breaks now!?"

- "Look at that! They're all burning! How could they... Damn you! Look at all the damage you've done!"

- "Your death will teach Cao Cao the pain of losing one of his own kin!"

- "You... You're an interesting one."

- "I shall be your opponent."

- "I don't know anything about dancing or performing, but the way you move on the battlefield is beautiful."

- "I see no end to this chaos. It makes me saddest for the children. All they know since birth is fighting, fighting."

- "You think your wife will haul me over the coals if I damage that pretty face of yours?"

- "Look at this fool. I expect a certain standard of intelligence from my enemies, y'know."

- "Look at you. So you listened to my advice and started growing your hair out?"

- "Hey, Xiahou Yuan. You don't shy from the limelight, do you! You go quite wild in battle!"

- "It's an ambush, alright. My lord, I will take control of the situation now."

See also: Xiahou Yuan/Quotes

"That's right! I did it! Now quit whining, you little punk!"

"You old man! You'll be joining all of those ships at the bottom of the river!"
~~Xiahou Yuan and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors 5

~~Xiahou Yuan and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors 5

"That hatred's gonna get you killed kid - by me!"
~~Ma Chao and Xiahou Yuan; Dynasty Warriors 5: Xtreme Legends

~~Ma Chao and Xiahou Yuan; Dynasty Warriors 5: Xtreme Legends

"What? You startled me. You're just like your father - I can never tell what you're talking about."

"That dichotomy of humor and strategy... Your indefatigable doggedness... That's what I'm talking about."

"Umm... I can't tell if you're praising me or making fun of me."
~~Cao Pi and Xiahou Yuan; Dynasty Warriors 7

~~Cao Pi and Xiahou Yuan; Dynasty Warriors 7

"Hey, now this isn't fair. How could I possibly hurt a beautiful young lady?"
~~Diaochan and Xiahou Yuan; Dynasty Warriors Next

~~Diaochan and Xiahou Yuan; Dynasty Warriors Next

"You honor me with your praise, General. So, let's show them our beauty together!"

"Me too...? No thanks. That's not gonna happen."

"I have seen your beauty, General. Come now, just dance as your heart desires!"

"O-Okay... I guess I could give it a try."
~~Xiahou Yuan and Zhang He; Dynasty Warriors 8

~~Xiahou Yuan and Zhang He; Dynasty Warriors 8

"We need to educate the children so they don't make the same mistakes as us. So they don't become warmongering fools."

"Sure, well, the best hing would be to bring an end to this chaos as quickly as possible. Make it easier for all."

"Ending the fighting won't necessarily achieve that. We need to change every element of this broken system."

"What, then? We have to teach them to fight during war, and then how to create a country once the war ends? I'm not very good with the details... but I know that we must set a good example to make them into fine and upright people!"
~~Xiahou Yuan and Sima Yi; Dynasty Warriors: Godseekers

~~Xiahou Yuan and Sima Yi; Dynasty Warriors: Godseekers

"Don't patronize me!"
~~Xiahou Yuan and Nagamasa; Warriors Orochi

~~Xiahou Yuan and Nagamasa; Warriors Orochi

"Hey! Take a look in the mirror before you start slinging the insults."
~~Hideyoshi and Xiahou Yuan; Warriors Orochi 2

~~Hideyoshi and Xiahou Yuan; Warriors Orochi 2

"This is as long as it gets. Why would I want to cover this beautiful face with any more hair than this?"

"Perhaps... Although, come to think of it, I never see any girls swooning for you either."
~~Xiahou Yuan and Magoichi; Warriors Orochi 3

~~Xiahou Yuan and Magoichi; Warriors Orochi 3

"That's right. I'm well known for my lightning fast attacks! I'm sorry, have I been stealing away attention from you?"

"Haha! The wilder the ones around him, the brighter the peacock shines! The more ruffians and oddballs in my entourage the better!"

"Hold on. You're classifying people now? Their types of wild behavior?"

"They aren't all just wild. Each has their own, carefully cultivated aesthetic."

"People's feelings, given form. Maybe I should look into dressing myself up a little more."

"Great idea! Xiahou Yuan, I'll handle everything! I'll create the wildest battle outfit you've ever seen."

"S-Sure. No need to go too wild."
~~Keiji and Xiahou Yuan; Warriors Orochi 4

~~Keiji and Xiahou Yuan; Warriors Orochi 4

"I cannot ask you to do-"

"My lord! What of the Mandate of Heaven? Who among us will quell this rebellion? I beg you to put your faith in a loyal servant."
~~Xiahou Yuan and Cao Cao; Kessen II

~~Xiahou Yuan and Cao Cao; Kessen II

sections.heading: Dynasty Warriors 9
sections.content:
『真・三國無双８』夏侯淵アクション動画

Xiahou Yuan is affiliated with the twin rods and bow and rod in this title. When equipped with them, Xiahou Yuan can perform the following unique attacks listed below.

Unique Flow Attack Finisher (Twin Rods): Enemy Downed +    : Crouches down and fires an arrow to the sky while generating a radial shockwave. Multiple arrows come raining down for a few seconds, giving Xiahou Yuan time to prepare for his next attack. Based on his first EX attack from the previous games.

Unique Flow Attack Finisher (Bow and Rod): Enemy Airborne +    : Xiahou Yuan's finisher has him fire three shots in a straight line before releasing two arrows that steadily travel in curved directions before converging to one spot, launching multiple foes to the air upon making impact.

Unique Trigger Attack (Twin Rods): R1 + : Hits enemies with a shoulder ram before sending them higher into the air with a crouching slam attack.

Unique Trigger Attack (Bow and Rod): R1 + : Hops back and fires a sonic-powered shot forward to knock targets down. Charging this move not only launches a stronger shot, but also causes Xiahou Yuan to fire his rod upward, causing multiple arrows to rain down in front of him. Derived from his first EX attack in the eighth title.

Special Technique: R1 + : Releases an ice-shattering shot strong enough to pierce through the sound barrier. The recoil from the attack forces Xiahou Yuan to stretch his shoulder momentarily per each use. Based on his second EX attack from the previous installment.

Musou Attack: : Performs a series of diagonal spin swipes while hopping along continuously. Xiahou Yuan then pulls out his bow and fires a wind-powered shot that sends foes flying away while leaving a brief air tunnel in its wake. Combines Xiahou Yuan's old Musou attack from the older games and his Power Shot Musou from the more recent ones.

Aerial Musou Attack:  : Shoots down several ice arrows in a swaying motion towards the front below. The barrage ends with rippling shot that freezes more enemies from a considerable distance. Considered an elemental variation of Xiahou Yuan's Arrowstorm Musou attack from past titles, though its trajectory cannot be maneuvered this time around.

Summon Horse: L2: Summon horse to the battlefield. Holding L2 as the horse approaches will allow the character to automatically mount them.

Auto-Run: Riding Mode + L2: Automatically runs towards a specified destination. If a destination has not been set, the horse will instead run towards the location of the player's currently selected mission.

Sprint: Riding Mode + Direction + R1: Causes horse to run at a faster pace. Doing so depletes their stamina each time.

Jump: Riding Mode + Direction + R2: Causes horse to jump high while running.

Horse Fast Attack: Riding Mode + : Performs a fast and seamless attack while mounted. The attack itself varies depending on what type of weapon is equipped and whether the enemy is grounded or airborne.

Horse Trigger Attack: Riding Mode + : Performs an attack that sends enemies flying into the air. May vary depending on what type of weapon is equipped.

Horse Musou Attack: Riding Mode + : Causes horse to sprint by consuming Musou instead of stamina.

Dismount Horse: Riding Mode + : Dismounts from horse and resumes default fighting stance.

Shooting Mode: 🡻: Pulls out bow and assumes archery stance. Can be cancelled by tapping 🡻 again.

Switch Arrows: 🡸 or 🡺: Switches between different types of arrows.

Bow Attack: Shooting Mode + : Fires current arrow at a target. Can lock on targets by tapping R3.

Lower Bow: Shooting Mode + : Lowers bow while squatting down.

sections.heading: Dynasty Warriors 8
sections.content: Xiahou Yuan now uses the bow and rod as his default weapon in this title.

sections.heading: Historical Information
sections.content:
Xiahou Yuan was a descendant of Han general Xiahou Ying and the younger cousin of Xiahou Dun, born in Qiao, Yu Province. He once took the blame of one of Cao Cao's offenses and was saved by the latter. In 190, Cao Cao rose an army in Chenliu to oppose the warlord Dong Zhuo. Xiahou Yuan joined him along with Xiahou Dun, Cao Ren and Cao Hong and became a major. He was afterwards promoted to Administrator of Chenliu and Yingchuan commanderies and participated in Cao Cao's battles during the 190's.

In 200, Cao Cao's forces clashed with Yuan Shao at Guandu. Xiahou Yuan was appointed "Colonel who Inspects the Army". After the battle was won, Xiahou Yuan was put in charge of transporting supplies from Yan, Yu and Xu Provinces to Cao Cao's army on the front lines, boosting his men's morale. In 206, Xiahou Yuan and Yu Jin put down the rebellion of Chang Xi, capturing 10 enemy garrisons and eventually forcing Chang Xi to surrender. Xiahou Yuan was subsequently promoted to "Colonel who Arranges the Army" and known for his swift movements in battle, earning him much praise from Cao Cao.

One year later, former Yellow Turban rebels led by Xu He and Sima Ju attacked cities and killed officials in Qing Province. Xiahou Yuan led Cao Cao's forces into battle and defeated the rebels, personally killing Xu He and capturing the enemy supplies, which he distributed among his troops. In 209, after the defeat at Chibi, Xiahou Yuan was sent to deal with the rebellion of Lei Xu in Lujiang and defeated him. He later joined Xu Huang in defeating the rebel leader Shang Yao in Bing Province and they massacred the population of the rebel base.

In 211, Xiahou Yuan joined Cao Cao in suppressing Ma Chao's revolt at Tong Gate and defeated him. Afterwards, he and Zhu Ling led their forces to pacify the Di tribes in Liang Province and joined Cao Cao at Anding, where they forced Yang Qiu to surrender. He was appointed "General who Protects the Army" and was put in charge of defending Chang'an. He defeated the bandit leader Liu Xiong and later besieged and killed Liang Xing at Hu.

In 213, Wei Kang was besieged by Ma Chao at Ji Castle. Xiahou Yuan led reinforcements but arrived too late. He fell for an ambush of Ma Chao and was forced to retreat to Chang'an. In 214, former generals of Ma Chao rebelled against him at Lucheng, killed his family and forced him to flee to Zhang Lu in Hanzhong. Ma Chao borrowed some troops and attacked Jiang Xu at Mt. Qi. Jiang Xu requested reinforcements and many of Xiahou Yuan's generals urged him to sent a messenger to Cao Cao for his approval, but Xiahou Yuan refused, saying that by the time the messenger would reach Cao Cao, Jiang Xu would be defeated. He sent Zhang He with 5,000 troops to aid him and Ma Chao was eventually defeated when Xiahou Yuan arrived with the rest of the army.

In 215, Xiahou Yuan battled Han Sui in Liang Province. Han Sui retreated to Xinggao and fortified the position. Xiahou Yuan decided to attack the Qiang city of Changli, which was the home of many of Han Sui's men. After raiding the city and setting fire to it, reinforcements from Xinggao arrived, as Xiahou Yuan had predicted. When his men wanted to build camps and fortifications to defend against Han Sui's army, Xiahou Yuan ordered them to attack Han Sui instead. Han Sui suffered a great defeat and died shortly afterwards. His men surrendered, while the Di chieftains fled to Ma Chao. Later that year, he defeated and killed the bandit leader Song Jian who rose in rebellion, pacifying most of Liang Province.

Xiahou Yuan participated in the campaign to subdue Zhang Lu in Hanzhong and was successful. He was stationed with Zhang He and Xu Huang in Hanzhong and ordered to pacify Ba Commandery. Xiahou Yuan was appointed "General who Attacks the West" and oversaw the affairs in Hanzhong. In 217, Liu Bei launched a campaign against Cao Cao in Hanzhong. Xiahou Yuan led his men to Yangping Gate and defended it from Liu Bei's assault. The two forces got into a stalemate for over a year.

Eventually, Liu Bei set fire to Xiahou Yuan's camp, so he sent Zhang He to deal with the enemy on the eastern side, while he himself covered the south. Zhang He was taunted into attacking Liu Bei and was defeated, so Xiahou Yuan sent a portion of his army to aid Zhang He. Following this, Liu Bei's general Huang Zhong led a fierce assault on Xiahou Yuan and his men, killing Xiahou Yuan in the process.

Xiahou Yuan received the posthumous title of "Marquis Min" (sympathetic marquis). His oldest son Xiahou Heng inherited his father's title and married a daughter of Cao Cao's younger brother. His second son Xiahou Ba became a general of Wei and later defected to Shu after Sima Yi's coup d'etat. His third son Xiahou Cheng died at the age of 18. His fourth son Xiahou Wei became a general of Wei as well. Xiahou Yuan's fifth son Xiahou Rong was by his father's side when the latter was killed. After Yuan's death, Xiahou Rong, although only 13 years old, took a sword and charged the enemy, leading to his own death. His sixth and seventh sons, Xiahou He and Xiahou Hui, served as officials of Wei.

sections.heading: Romance of the Three Kingdoms
sections.content:
In Romance of the Three Kingdoms, the nature of Xiahou Yuan's death is heavily dramatized. During that time, he and his men were stationed at Mt. Dingjun where they clashed against the forces of Huang Zhong. Believing that the enemy's position at Mt. Tiandang would give them the advantage in terrain, a worried Xiahou Yuan quickly led his troops and surrounded the area. This proved to be a fatal mistake as Fa Zheng noticed their fatigue and signaled Huang Zhong to begin the assault. Unable to react by this sudden move, Xiahou Yuan was sliced into half by his nemesis.

- Warriors series

- Romance of the Three Kingdoms

- Miscellaneous

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.content:
- His nickname with Japanese Dynasty Warriors fans is "Enjeru" (淵ジェル), a portmanteau of the Japanese way of saying Yuan (En) and angel. The Chinese-English equivalent would be "Yuangel".

- In his artworks and renders for Dynasty Warriors 4 and 5, Xiahou Yuan is rendered with his second weapon as opposed to his signature third weapon.
